trees.title: I want a reusable workflow
trees.blurb: Sub-agent vs skill vs custom slash command — easy to mix up.

options.label: Claude picks it up automatically when intent matches
I trigger it explicitly by typing /name

q-shape.prompt: What's the shape of the work?

options.label: A single prompt template — runs in my main session
A focused, multi-step worker with its own context

r-skill.title: Use a Skill
r-skill.summary: Skills auto-activate when their description matches the user's intent. Drop a SKILL.md in .claude/skills/<name>/. They can include helper scripts and files.

r-command.title: Use a Custom slash command
r-command.summary: Markdown file in .claude/commands/<name>.md. Frontmatter declares allowed tools, model, and arguments. Body is the prompt template.

r-subagent.title: Use a Custom sub-agent
r-subagent.summary: Markdown file in .claude/agents/<name>.md with name, description, tools, model. Claude routes work to it when the description matches; it runs in its own context.

trees.title: Where do my settings go?
trees.blurb: User vs project vs local — three scopes that deep-merge.

q-share.prompt: Should this apply to your teammates too?

options.label: Yes — committed in the repo for everyone

q-secret.prompt: Does it contain secrets (API keys, tokens)?

q-personal.prompt: Should it apply to every project, or just this repo?

r-user.title: User scope — ~/.claude/settings.json
r-user.summary: Loaded for every Claude Code session on your machine. Use for personal preferences and globally-applicable hooks.

r-project.title: Project scope — <repo>/.claude/settings.json (committed)
r-project.summary: Shared with the team. Hooks, MCP servers, conventions everyone in the repo should get.

r-local.title: Local scope — <repo>/.claude/settings.local.json (gitignored)
r-local.summary: Per-machine, never committed. Right place for secrets, personal experiments, machine-specific paths. Wins over project + user on conflicts.

trees.title: How should I automate this?
trees.blurb: Hook vs /loop vs /schedule — depends on the trigger.

options.label: On every tool call / edit / session boundary
On a fixed interval while I'm working
On a clock / cron schedule, even when I'm offline

r-hook.title: Use a Hook
r-hook.summary: Configured in settings.json. Fires deterministically on lifecycle events — no model trust required. Best for formatting, blocking, logging, notifications.

r-loop.title: Use /loop
r-loop.summary: Re-runs a prompt or command on an interval inside your active session. Stop with /loop-stop. Good for monitoring while you work.

r-schedule.title: Use /schedule
r-schedule.summary: Cron-scheduled remote agent. Runs in the cloud on a clock — perfect for one-time follow-ups or recurring weekly work.

trees.title: How careful should this session be?
trees.blurb: Plan vs Accept Edits vs Default — pick the right safety level.

q-stakes.prompt: What's the goal of this session?

options.label: Explore an idea, decide an approach — no edits yet
Execute a clear task — many edits expected

r-plan.title: Plan mode
r-plan.summary: Read-only. Claude proposes a plan, can't edit files or run commands. Approve the plan to execute. Toggle with Shift+Tab or /plan.

r-accept.title: Accept Edits mode
r-accept.summary: Auto-approves Edit/Write for the rest of the session. Bash and risky tools still ask. Toggle with Shift+Tab.

r-default.title: Default mode
r-default.summary: Each tool call asks for approval based on your permission rules. The right starting point — switch into Plan or Accept Edits when scope clarifies.
